zotero.abstractNote: In systems neuroscience, the ability to combine data across experiments is crucial for building robust models of neural function. However, inter-experimental variability—often caused by differences in recording conditions, animal preparations, or equipment—can obscure the underlying biological signals. Here, we present a method to remove inter-experimental variability from functional data. Our approach is based on a generative model that disentangles biological signals from experimental noise. We demonstrate that our method effectively removes inter-experimental variability while preserving the relevant biological information, leading to improved performance in downstream analysis tasks such as cell type classification and neural encoding modeling.
